mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in FEDFUNDS raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, so larger 3-month increases in FEDFUNDS predict lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 3.08 for 2022-10-01 after rapid 2022 tightening
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS do not produce lower subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, with walk-forward Rank IC >= -0.03 failing to hold over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum crosses above a high threshold, investors price additional policy tightening and margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional mean returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing expected earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion transitioning toward labor-market slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns after the UNRATE trigger are not lower than baseline returns, or if the trigger has a nonnegative walk-forward Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When nominal RSAFS growth lags CPIAUCSL growth, real retail demand is contracting, which reduces revenue expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses below CPIAUCSL 3-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 683974.0 for 2022-09-01 while CPIAUCSL was elevated
regime_hint: inflation squeeze on real household demand
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-minus-CPIAUCSL real-demand trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or has Rank IC above -0.03 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive INDPRO momentum signals expanding physical output and operating leverage, so stronger 6-month growth in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 6-month INDPRO growth fails to rank positively with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns, defined as walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST during high FEDFUNDS periods transmits rate tightening into construction, durable-goods demand, and credit sensitivity, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month growth crosses below -10% while FEDFUNDS is above 3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 1439.0 for 2022-09-01 and FEDFUNDS was 3.08 for 2022-10-01
regime_hint: rate-sensitive housing downturn during monetary tightening
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST and FEDFUNDS trigger is not followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if adding FEDFUNDS to HOUST does not improve out-of-sample classification accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS continues to rise and UNRATE does not rise, household income remains resilient and recession risk stays contained, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is less than 0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 153308.0 and UNRATE vintage value was 3.7 for 2022-10-01
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns at a frequency no higher than the unconditional positive rate, or if the interaction term has nonpositive out-of-sample contribution.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum falls while FEDFUNDS is already restrictive, markets price a slower path of future hikes and discount-rate relief, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth falls more than 2 percentage points below its prior 6-month annualized growth while FEDFUNDS is above 3%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL remained high and FEDFUNDS was 3.08 at the 2022-10-01 vintage
regime_hint: restrictive policy regime approaching an inflation-driven pause
failure_condition: Reject if the disinflation-with-restrictive-FEDFUNDS trigger does not raise 5-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional mean, or if triggered hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.
